summary: The diff demonstrates partial implementation of the BuildRequest. Icon replacements and additions (REQ-1, REQ-2, REQ-3) are fully implemented with proper asset paths and toolbar integration. Arrow rotation infrastructure (REQ-4) is partially implemented with state management and UI handlers, but complete rotation interaction logic is truncated and cannot be fully verified. Export as PNG removal (REQ-5) shows removal from toolbar and page components, but the diff does not confirm complete removal of all export logic and dependencies due to truncation.

missing_requirements.requirement: Enable click-and-drag rotation for arrows in the attack path visualizer. Users should be able to click on an arrow and drag to freely rotate it around its connection points.
missing_requirements.reason: While arrow rotation state management and onUpdateArrowRotation handler are present, the actual click-and-drag rotation interaction logic in AttackPathCanvas.tsx is truncated at line ~200, preventing verification that the complete drag-to-rotate implementation exists. The acceptance criteria require arrows to be rotatable by dragging with natural and responsive interaction, which cannot be confirmed from the truncated code.
